channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Foxconn confirms cyberattack claimed by Nitrogen ransomware gang
item.description: Foxconn, the world's largest electronics manufacturer, says some of its North American factories are now working to resume normal operations after a cyberattack. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 73 Seconds to Breach, 24 Hours to Patch: The Case for Autonomous Validation
item.description: Attackers can compromise systems in minutes while patching and response still take hours or days. Picus Security breaks down why autonomous validation is becoming critical for modern defense strategie...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft says some users can't install Office on Windows 365 devices
item.description: Microsoft says some customers are experiencing issues downloading and installing Office on their Windows 365 devices. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: [Webinar] Why Your AppSec Tools Miss the "Lethal Path" (and How to Fix It)
item.description: TL;DR: Stop chasing thousands of "toast" alerts. Join experts from Wiz and Okta/GitLab to learn how hackers connect tiny flaws to build a "Lethal Chain" to your data—and how to break it. Register for ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Most Remediation Programs Never Confirm the Fix Actually Worked
item.description: Security teams have never had better visibility into their environments and never been worse at confirming what they fix stays fixed. Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report puts the mean time to exploit at a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities spanning its product portfolio, although none of them have been listed as publicly known or under active attack. Of the 138 flaws...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GemStuffer Abuses 150+ RubyGems to Exfiltrate Scraped U.K. Council Portal Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign dubbed GemStuffer that has targeted the RubyGems repository with more than 150 gems that use the registry as a data exfiltration chann...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Android Adds Intrusion Logging for Sophisticated Spyware Forensics
item.description: Google on Tuesday unveiled a new opt-in Android feature called Intrusion Logging for storing forensic logs to better analyze sophisticated spyware attacks. Intrusion Logging, available as part of Adva...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US govt seeks Instructure testimony on massive Canvas cyberattack
item.description: The U.S. House Committee on Homeland Security is calling on Instructure executives to testify about two cyberattacks by the ShinyHunters extortion group that targeted the company's Canvas platform, al...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
item.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft & Not a Zero-Day In Sight
item.description: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: UK fines water supplier $1.3M for exposing data of 664k customers
item.description: The Information Commissioner's Office has fined South Staffordshire Water Plc and parent company South Staffordshire Plc £963,900 ($1.3 million) over a cyberattack that exposed the personal data of 66...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webinar: Fixing the gaps in network incident response
item.description: IT teams often struggle to quickly coordinate responses across disparate systems during network incidents. This upcoming webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can reduce response t...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Signal adds security warnings for social engineering, phishing attacks
item.description: Signal has introduced new in-app confirmations and warning messages as additional safeguards against phishing and social engineering attempts that could lead to various forms of fraud. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update to fix the May 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities and resolve an issue with the new Remote Desktop warnings. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fortinet warns of critical RCE flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
item.description: Fortinet has released security patches for two critical vulnerabilities in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could enable attackers to run commands or arbitrary code. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows 11 KB5089549 & KB5087420 cumulative updates released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5089549 and KB5087420 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws, no zero-days
item.description: Today is Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday, with security updates for 120 flaws and no zero-days disclosed this month. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
item.description: Škoda Auto, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Volkswagen Group, has disclosed a data breach after attackers hacked its online shop and stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of custome...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Android 17 to expand banking scam call and privacy protections
item.description: Android 17, expected to roll out next month, will introduce several security and privacy features focused on device theft, threat detection, and banking scam calls. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Exim BDAT Vulnerability Exposes GnuTLS Builds to Potential Code Execution
item.description: Exim has released security updates to address a severe security issue affecting certain configurations that could enable memory corruption and potential code execution. Exim is an open-source Mail Tra...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: RubyGems Suspends New Signups After Hundreds of Malicious Packages Are Uploaded
item.description: RubyGems, the standard package manager for the Ruby programming language, has temporarily paused account sign ups following what has been described as a "major malicious attack." "We're dealing with a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
item.description: A tokenizer library file present in Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack the model's outputs and exfiltrate data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new version of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that uses The Open Network (TON) for command-and-control (C2). The new variant, observed by ThreatFabric betw...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 20 Leaders Who Built the CISO Era: 2 Decades of Change
item.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary special coverage, we profile the CISOs, founders, researchers, criminals, and policymakers who rewrote the enterprise risk playbook....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar: What the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered - and How Radiant Security Can Help
item.description: Why do the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered? Security operations teams are drowning in alerts. But the real problem isn't always alert volume; it's the blind spots. The most dangerous alerts are the ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recentsupply chain attack spree, has been linked to the compromise of the npm and PyPI packages from TanStack, UiPath, Mistral AI, OpenSearch, and Guardrails AI as...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Shai Hulud attack ships signed malicious TanStack, Mistral npm packages
item.description: Hundreds of packages across npm and PyPI have been compromised in a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain campaign delivering credential-stealing malware targeting developers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
item.description: Hundreds of npm packages infected by the self-propagating, credential-stealing worm from TeamPCP are related to the open source TanStack ecosystem....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: SAP fixes critical vulnerabilities in Commerce Cloud and S/4HANA
item.description: SAP has released the May 2026 security updates addressing 15 vulnerabilities across multiple products, including two critical flaws in the Commerce Cloud enterprise-grade e-commerce platform and the S...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
